goal: Generate a clean academic landscape architecture diagram for the full reusable observation-level framework from production video to digital-twin-ready state variables.

style.visual_language: clean academic schematic with sparse labels and balanced spacing
style.line_style: thin to medium outlines with straight arrows
style.effects: flat vector only, no gradients, no 3D, no glossy UI

article_context.paper_theme: observation-level framework for digital twins in manufacturing
article_context.input_domain: single production-camera video streams in a real manufacturing facility
article_context.final_output: digital twin interface with state variables, dashboards, reports, and decision support

instructions: Use a left-to-right flow with rounded rectangles and thin arrows.
Keep the figure technical, sparse, and publication-ready.
Show reusable framework layers rather than a single local case study.
Show candidate methods inside the detection and tracking layers, but visually highlight the selected components.
Make the observation variable layer explicit because it is the conceptual contribution.
Use subtle color coding by layer and keep text short.

nodes.label:
Detection Layer
YOLO-family candidates
selected: YOLO11x

Tracking Layer
ByteTrack / BoT-SORT
selected: ByteTrack

Observation Variables
occupancy, entries, exits,
dwell time, trajectories, heatmaps

Digital Twin Interface
state variables, dashboards,
reports, decision support

avoid: camera ID labels such as CAM10
caption_hint: Reusable observation-level framework for digital twins in manufacturing, connecting production video streams, perception, tracking, spatial semantics, and derived observation variables to digital twin interfaces.
